forces.title: 💪 Forces and Movement
forces.html:
Things move when a force acts on them. A force is a push or a pull. When you kick a ball, you push it. When you open a door, you pull it. Both are forces that create movement.

Gravity is a force that pulls everything down to the ground. When you throw a ball up in the air, gravity pulls it back down. That is why we do not float away!

Forces can change the speed of things. A strong push makes a ball go fast. A gentle push makes it go slow. Brakes on a bicycle use force to stop the wheels. When you press the brakes, you slow down and stop.

Some things are easy to push and some are hard. A light ball is easier to push than a heavy one. A big box is harder to move than a small box. The heavier something is, the more force you need to move it.

We use forces every day! When you use a computer mouse, you push it. When you swipe a tablet, you push the screen. Forces are everywhere!

everyday.title: 🏃 Forces in Everyday Life
everyday.html:
We use push and pull forces all the time. Think about a suitcase with wheels. You pull the suitcase behind you at the airport. It is easier to pull a suitcase with wheels than to carry it!

In sports, we use forces too. When you throw a ball, you push it with your hand. A football player kicks the ball — that is a push with the foot. A tennis player hits the ball with a racket — that is also a push.

Cars use brakes to stop. The brakes push against the wheels and the car slows down. Without brakes, cars could not stop! That is why brakes are very important for safety.

Is a car faster than a bicycle? Yes! A car has more speed. Is a bicycle faster than walking? Yes! A bicycle is faster than walking but slower than a car.

A tablet is lighter than a computer. A feather is lighter than a book. A suitcase is heavier than a backpack. We use comparisons every day to describe the world around us!

Forces are amazing! Every time you push a door, pull a drawer, or throw a ball, you are using forces. The ground pushes back on your feet when you jump. Forces make movement happen!
